name: silent-spec
description: Auto-generates unit tests on file save using AI — silent, surgical, zero friction

untrustedWorkspaces.description: Automatic test generation and TypeScript compilation checks require a trusted workspace. In untrusted workspaces, generation is disabled.

commands.title: SilentSpec: Find Gaps in Current File
SilentSpec: Generate Tests for Current File

silentspec.enabled.description: Enable or disable SilentSpec auto test generation

silentspec.supportedExtensions.description: List of file extensions that SilentSpec will generate tests for

enumDescriptions: Free local generation via Ollama — no API key needed
Claude (Anthropic) — best quality, requires API key
GitHub Models — free tier, requires GitHub Personal Access Token
Azure OpenAI — requires Azure endpoint, deployment, and API key
OpenAI-compatible API — works with Groq, Together, Fireworks, DeepSeek, and similar providers
AWS Bedrock — supports Claude models on Bedrock
Google Vertex AI — supports Gemini and Claude on Vertex
silentspec.provider.description: AI provider for test generation. Use 'ollama' for free local generation.

silentspec.model.description: Model override. Leave empty for provider default.
silentspec.model.markdownDescription: Legacy model override for Ollama, Claude, OpenAI, and GitHub Models. Leave empty to use provider defaults. Azure, Bedrock, Vertex, compat, and vLLM use their provider-specific model/deployment settings.

silentspec.openSpecOnCreate.description: Open the generated spec file in a split panel when first created.

silentspec.aiTimeoutSeconds.description: Timeout in seconds for AI provider calls. Increase for slow connections.

silentspec.maxFunctionsPerRun.description: Hard cap on functions per AI call. Adaptive logic auto-reduces to 5 for files >12k chars (important for GitHub Models free tier). Set to 3-5 for free-tier providers, 8 for paid APIs.

silentspec.azure.endpoint.description: Azure OpenAI resource endpoint, for example https://your-resource.openai.azure.com
silentspec.azure.endpoint.markdownDescription: Azure OpenAI resource endpoint. Must be an HTTPS Azure resource URL ending in `openai.azure.com` or `cognitiveservices.azure.com`, for example `https://your-resource.openai.azure.com`.

silentspec.azure.deploymentName.description: Azure OpenAI deployment name to use for chat completions.
silentspec.azure.deploymentName.markdownDescription: Your Azure OpenAI deployment name. Must match exactly what you named it in the **Azure AI Foundry portal** under Deployments. Example: if you created a deployment called `my-gpt4`, enter `my-gpt4` here.

silentspec.compat.baseUrl.description: Base URL for an OpenAI-compatible provider.
silentspec.compat.baseUrl.markdownDescription: Base URL for a supported HTTPS OpenAI-compatible provider. Allowed hosted providers: Groq (`https://api.groq.com/openai/v1`), Together AI (`https://api.together.xyz/v1`), Fireworks AI (`https://api.fireworks.ai/inference/v1`), and DeepSeek (`https://api.deepseek.com/v1`).

silentspec.compat.model.description: Model name for the OpenAI-compatible provider.

silentspec.compat.providerLabel.description: Display label for the OpenAI-compatible provider in messages and the status bar.

silentspec.vllm.baseUrl.description: Base URL for a local vLLM server.
silentspec.vllm.baseUrl.markdownDescription: Base URL for a local vLLM server. Must point to `localhost`, `127.0.0.1`, or `[::1]`; remote vLLM URLs are blocked because source code is sent in the request body.

silentspec.vllm.autoDetect.description: Auto-detect a local vLLM server on activation when no provider is explicitly configured.

silentspec.bedrock.modelId.description: Bedrock Claude model ID to invoke.
silentspec.bedrock.modelId.markdownDescription: Bedrock model ID for Claude models. Must be enabled in AWS Console > Bedrock > Model Access. Example: `anthropic.claude-3-5-sonnet-20241022-v2:0`. V1.1 supports Claude-on-Bedrock only; other model families require additional dispatch logic.

silentspec.bedrock.profile.description: AWS shared credentials profile name when auth mode is profile.

silentspec.bedrock.authMode.description: Authentication mode for Bedrock: default AWS chain, shared profile, or static credentials stored in SecretStorage.

silentspec.vertex.projectId.description: Google Cloud project ID for Vertex AI.

silentspec.vertex.location.markdownDescription: Vertex AI location, such as `us-central1` or `europe-west4`. Must be a valid GCP region string; arbitrary hostnames or paths are rejected.

silentspec.vertex.model.description: Vertex AI model name. Supports Gemini models and Claude models on Vertex.
silentspec.vertex.model.markdownDescription: Vertex AI model name. Examples: `gemini-2.0-flash`, `gemini-1.5-pro`, `claude-3-5-sonnet@20241022`. Claude models on Vertex require Model Garden enablement and Anthropic terms acceptance.

silentspec.vertex.serviceAccountKeyPath.description: Path to a Vertex AI service account key JSON file when not using ADC.

silentspec.vertex.useADC.description: Use Application Default Credentials for Vertex AI instead of a key file.

scripts.compile: npm run check-types && npm run lint && node esbuild.js
scripts.package: npm run check-types && npm run lint && node esbuild.js --production
scripts.pretest: npm run compile-tests && npm run compile && npm run lint
scripts.test:e2e: npm run compile-tests && npm run compile && vscode-test
